mismatch_files.summary: Comment incorrectly calls the time-colon separator a 'thousands separator'; it is a time separator (hours:minutes format), not digit grouping.

issues.actually_tests: The --time-colon flag converts decimal fractional hours to HH:MM format (e.g. 12345.50h → 12345:30h). The colon in the output is a time separator between hours and minutes, not a thousands separator. Thousands separators group digits (like commas in 12,345), which is a completely different concept.

The --time-colon option converts all decimal hour values to HH:MM format regardless of digit count (e.g. 1.5h → 1:30h works the same way). The claim that colon separator is only triggered for values > 3 digits is likely inaccurate unless there is a special code path in amount.cc specifically for large values.
